purpose: Evaluation corpus for joint-overlay registration features: successful registrations, nearby offset negatives, hard harmonically-compatible negatives, and multiple mashup technique families, across song pairs. Labels are evaluation truth for leave-one-song-pair-out testing — they must NEVER be read by discovery or probe code as inputs. See docs/experiment-joint-registration-features.md.

label_taxonomy.success: a registration a human auditioned (or attested) as musically working
label_taxonomy.near_offset_negative: a small whole-bar shift of a success (e.g. -1/-2/-3 bars) presumed or judged not to work
label_taxonomy.hard_harmonic_negative: harmonically compatible by per-track analysis but judged not to work in combination
label_taxonomy.random_negative: an arbitrary registration with no reason to work

label_states.annotated: labeled from human audition or explicit user attestation (method required)
label_states.hypothesis: presumed label awaiting audition — usable to plan auditions, NEVER as evaluation truth

honesty_notes: n=1 pair as of 2026-07-11: no cross-pair claim of any kind is supported by this corpus yet.
Most near-offset negatives on the first pair are hypotheses, not auditioned negatives — auditioning them is the highest-value labeling work.

Offsets are in the anchor frame: guest bar 1 delayed N host bars past the first-stable-downbeat coincidence. Labels attach to the stated conform (grid/pitch); a different conform is a different audition.
Do not commit audio; local_path entries are machine-local and may be missing — skip, never fail.

2026-07-14: first grounded window audition contradicts the witness at its own offset — viability is window/arrangement-scoped, not a global property of a registration. Schema v2 should attach labels to (registration, window).

host.title: Skyfall (Adele)
host.notes: structural foundation; metrical tempo ~74-76 (half-time reading of tracked ~148)

shared_grid_bpm.note: witnessed working point; ~74-90 region hypothesized viable

pitch_shift_semitones.note: verify from session; discovery chroma rotation independently proposed +2

registrations.label: success
registrations.note: CONFLICT ON RECORD: witnessed as a working construction in the full djay arrangement (muted intro, entrance ~guest bar 8, convincing chorus 2 -> final chorus), but judged NOT viable in the bare 8-bar early window (rhythm 5, harmony 5, phrase/section 1, confidence high; blinded_audition:anchor_neighborhood_v2). Viability appears window/arrangement-scoped, not registration-global. Label kept success for the arrangement witness; the window judgment is evaluation truth for that window. window-scoped: bare 8-bar overlay, host bars 8-16 (0-based anchor frame), v2 render; judged 2026-07-14

wanted: at least one hard_harmonic_negative registration (audition a chroma-admissible offset that fails by ear)
audition of -1/-2/-3 and +1/+2/+3 neighbors of success@0

window_auditions.render: v2 (single phase-vocoder pass + soxr_hq resample, 44.1 kHz)
window_auditions.summary: all offsets -3..+3 judged non-viable (or unsure at -3): rhythmic and harmonic coherence 5/5 at EVERY offset, phrase/section coherence 1 at every offset (2 at -3), masking 3, confidence high
window_auditions.instrument_caveat: a constant guest phrase-boundary extraction error would produce this same all-offsets phrase-1 pattern; a guest-only reference clip is needed to rule that out before treating the phrase-1 scores as fact about the overlay

wanted_pairs: a second overlay pair from a different genre pairing
at least one transition_blend-family pair (different technique family)
one deliberately incompatible pair (all registrations expected negative)
